workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_tiqora/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in the configured communication_language.
Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps.

step.action: Load project config from {project_config} if present (optional for context fetch — warn if missing but continue).
Load profile config from {profile_config} when available (optional).
Build effective config as: profile config (base) + project config (override).
Check for {workspace_config}. If found, load it and extract the list of all repos (name, role, localPath).
Set {{is_multi_repo}} = true. List all repos as {{workspace_repos}}.
Run: git log --oneline -10 → capture last 10 commits as {{recent_commits}}.
Run: git status --short → capture unstaged/staged changes as {{git_status}}.
For each repo in {{workspace_repos}}, capture current branch and git status if accessible.

step.critical: Context documents live in each repo's own .tiqora/context/ — not centralized. Load from each repo independently.

check.action: For each repo in {{workspace_repos}}: - Check {repo.localPath}/.tiqora/context/ for: - architecture.md → append to {{arch_contexts}} with repo name as prefix - qa-reviews/ → note presence and count - dev-notes/ → note presence and count - incidents/ → note presence and count - Note last modified date for freshness assessment

step.action: Note which context documents exist and their approximate freshness (last modified date if available).
step.output: [CONTEXT] ✓ Existing docs: {{list of found docs per repo or "none"}}

check.if: a ticket ID was provided by the user or calling workflow
check.action: Fetch ticket details via configured PM MCP connector: {{ticketId}}.
Capture: title, description, acceptance criteria, status, labels, assignee as {{ticket_context}}.
Log warning: "⚠ Could not fetch ticket {{ticketId}}. Continuing without ticket context."
